ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-23-2010 Continued To May-18-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 291645 The Estate of Adalbert Bartal and with case number 292097 The Estate of Albert Clifton Bartal.

Appearances: Francisco Torres for Mike Pryzvieski. Gary Lieberman for Albert Bartal. Barbara Krivosh (Creditor) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that the 41st Avenue property was listed as separate property, that half would go to Sadie and half to Albert, and that the petition did not state the disposition of the property. Mr. Lieberman stated that the 41st Avenue property was the only property in Adalbert's estate, that there was an error in the order, that he would file an ex parte petition to amend the order and that the Spousal Property petition should be for the 41st Avenue property. The Court pointed out that the escrow statement submitted was for the Fulton St. property. Mr. Lieberman stated that the Fulton St. property is in Sadie's estate and that it was sold as part of Sadie's estate. The Court stated that the issues needed to be cleared up, that proper property descriptions were needed, and suggested addressing Adalbert's estate first to clear up the issue with regard to the 41st Avenue property and the Fulton St. property. All three matters were continued to May 18, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Lieberman will amend the Spousal Property Petition.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
